content:
Case 17:
Initial presentation:
- Age: 44
- Sex: male
- Chief complaint: Scheduled follow-up after alloSCT and CD19 CAR-T for B-ALL, with interest in outpatient treatment options
- Vital signs: Temp 36.9 °C, BP not reported at this visit, HR not reported at this visit, RR not reported at this visit, SpO₂ not reported at this visit.
Setting: Outpatient hematology/oncology follow-up after alloSCT and CAR-T
Capabilities: You are evaluating a clinically stable patient in a routine follow-up setting after intensive leukemia therapy. You can review prior history and recent clinical course, perform a targeted examination, and request standard follow-up evaluations appropriate to an ambulatory visit. Decisions focus on surveillance and whether further workup for relapse or complications is indicated.
Provide either a request for a single information item or solve.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "detailed clinical history including timeline of B-ALL diagnosis, alloSCT, CAR-T therapy, and current disease status"}}

response.request: detailed clinical history including timeline of B-ALL diagnosis, alloSCT, CAR-T therapy, and current disease status

content:
'treatment_history' available in round 3.
This maps to the detailed treatment history (prior leukemia therapies, alloSCT, CAR‑T) which is recorded as 'treatment_history' but is not available until a later round. In the meantime you can request interval history or exam findings (history_physical) or demographics/performance status for available context.
(4 invalid requests remaining before case is cancelled. Consider solving with available information or requesting a different item.)
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "history_physical"}}

History Physical:
2024-07-04: The patient presented for scheduled follow-up, reporting stable overall condition. He denied fever, night sweats, or weight loss. No exertional dyspnea, abdominal pain, or neurological complaints were reported. The patient informed the team that he had stopped some medications. He actively inquired about “next treatment options,” emphasizing his preference for outpatient-based regimens.
Current medication: aciclovir 400 mg 1-0-1, posaconazole stopped, 1-0-0, cotrimoxazole 960 mg three times per week (taken irregularly), bisoprolol 2.5 mg 1-0-1, ramipril 5 mg 1-0-1, amlodipine 5 mg 1-0-1, atorvastatin 10 mg (stopped), folic acid 5 mg 1-0-0, magnesium 300 mg 1-0-0, and various nutritional supplements.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "complete blood count with differential"}}

Cbc Chemistry:
Hb 10.6 g/dL, WBC 3.1 × 10⁹/L, Plt 98 × 10⁹/L; MCV 92 fL, MCH 29 pg.
Differential: neutrophils 42%, lymphocytes 48%, monocytes 7%, eosinophils 2%, basophils 1%.
Coagulation: INR 1.1, aPTT 30 sec.
Chemistry: Na 138 mmol/L, K 4.5 mmol/L, creatinine 1.4 mg/dL, BUN 19 mg/dL, AST 28 U/L, ALT 32 U/L, bilirubin 0.9 mg/dL, LDH 220 U/L, uric acid 5.2 mg/dL, CRP 0.6 mg/dL.
Cardiac biomarkers: NT-proBNP 460 pg/mL, troponin T 0.012 ng/mL.

'treatment_history' not available. Request a specific test or report.
This item is available in a later round. In the current round, you can request demographics, history and physical, CBC/chemistry, or baseline imaging instead.
(4 invalid requests remaining before case is cancelled. Consider solving with available information or requesting a different item.)
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "baseline imaging"}}

Baseline Imaging:
2024-07-09: Abdominal ultrasound showed normal liver and spleen size, no lymphadenopathy, and no ascites.
Echocardiography demonstrated preserved left ventricular function (LVEF 62%), without pericardial effusion.
Sinus CT revealed bilateral maxillary sinus opacification with suspicion of fungal superinfection.

In the current round you can request routine blood tests (CBC/chemistry) or the available history/physical or baseline imaging instead.
(4 invalid requests remaining before case is cancelled. Consider solving with available information or requesting a different item.)
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "demographics and performance status"}}

Demographics:
Age: 44 years
Sex: Male
Performance status: ECOG 0
Comorbidities: Arterial hypertension, hyperlipidemia, mild glucose intolerance. History of acute leukemia and allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation.
Social history: Married, living with wife and 16-year-old daughter. Has three siblings; both parents are deceased. History of prior drug abuse. The patient maintains unusual lifestyle habits, including waking daily at 02:30 a.m. for cold-water swimming. Diet consists of multiple protein supplements (eight types) and approximately 15 hard-boiled eggs per week. Known for refusing inpatient treatment.
Height: 181 cm; Weight: 84 kg

solve.working_diagnosis: B-cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia (B-ALL) in remission following alloSCT and CD19 CAR-T therapy, complicated by suspected invasive fungal sinusitis and medication non-adherence in a post-transplant immunocompromised patient
differentials: Post-transplant marrow recovery with residual cytopenias
Early relapsed B-ALL (would require bone marrow evaluation to exclude)
Chronic graft-versus-host disease contributing to cytopenias

treatment_plan.action: Urgent ENT consultation with nasal endoscopy and biopsy of sinus lesions to evaluate for invasive fungal sinusitis (Aspergillus, Mucor)
Reinitiate posaconazole 300mg daily or switch to voriconazole given high suspicion for fungal sinusitis and patient's discontinuation of antifungal prophylaxis
Reinforce importance of prophylactic medications - particularly cotrimoxazole for PJP prophylaxis and antifungal agents; provide patient education on infection risks post-transplant/CAR-T
Schedule bone marrow biopsy with flow cytometry and MRD assessment to evaluate disease status and confirm ongoing remission
Monitor renal function given creatinine 1.4 mg/dL before initiating azole antifungals; consider dose adjustment
Continue surveillance with serial CBCs monthly and comprehensive metabolic panels to monitor cytopenias and renal function

content: {"response": {"action": "request", "request": "bone_marrow"}}

Bone Marrow:
Bone marrow biopsy (2024-07-09): The marrow was hypocellular (cellularity 25%) with therapy-related toxic changes. Trilineage hematopoiesis was present but displayed dysplastic features: megakaryopoiesis was reduced with small, hypolobulated forms; erythropoiesis showed megaloblastoid maturation; and granulopoiesis exhibited toxic granulation and nuclear irregularities. There was no blast excess (<5% by morphology).
Flow cytometry revealed a normal distribution of myeloid and lymphoid populations with T-cells showing mild inversion of the CD4/CD8 ratio. A small blast population (0.1%) was detected, characterized by CD19-/CD22-, CD34^dim, CD33-, CD10-, CD20-, CD117+, CD5+, CD7+, CD56-, cyMPO-, cyTdT-, cyCD3-.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "treatment_history"}}

'treatment_history' available in round 3.
This item is scheduled for a later round and is not available now. In this round you can request items like bone_marrow, cytogenetics_chimerism, or extended_diagnostics for relevant diagnostic details.
(4 invalid requests remaining before case is cancelled. Consider solving with available information or requesting a different item.)
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "cytogenetics_chimerism"}}

Cytogenetics Chimerism:
Karyotype: 46, XX.
Chimerism: 0.06% recipient.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "extended_diagnostics"}}

Extended Diagnostics:
ENT consultation (2024-07-13) with endoscopic sinus surgery revealed a fungal mass, which was excised completely. Culture grew Aspergillus fumigatus (azole-sensitive). Histology showed typical hyphae. Following infectious disease consultation, antifungal therapy was switched to isavuconazole for outpatient use, and liposomal amphotericin B was reserved for inpatient administration.
Pulmonary function testing showed a mildly reduced DLCO (65% predicted).
Blood cultures were negative.
Viral PCR monitoring: CMV, EBV, and adenovirus all negative.
Serology: HBV, HCV, and HIV negative.
Immunologic profile: CD4⁺ 110/µL, CD8⁺ 320/µL (ratio 0.34); IgG 220 mg/dL.
LP was performed: cerebrospinal fluid was clear and colorless. Cell count 2/µL; protein 42 mg/dL; glucose 68 mg/dL. Cytospin and morphology showed no blasts.
FDG-PET/CT (2024-07-11) demonstrated no hypermetabolic lymphadenopathy or extranodal disease. There was intense FDG uptake in both maxillary sinuses. Previously noted abdominal subcutaneous nodules had resolved. No abnormal uptake was observed in the liver, spleen, bone marrow, or CNS. Mild symmetrical uptake in paraspinal and thigh muscles was attributed to recent physical activity.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "B-ALL in morphologic remission post alloSCT and CD19 CAR-T therapy with confirmed invasive pulmonary/sinus aspergillosis and severe secondary immunodeficiency (cellular and humoral), with a small aberrant blast population (0.1%) requiring close MRD surveillance for potential CD19-negative relapse", "differentials": ["CD19-negative lineage switch relapse of B-ALL (antigen escape post CAR-T)", "Therapy-related myelodysplastic changes without relapse", "Early mixed phenotype acute leukemia emergence"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Continue isavuconazole antifungal therapy for invasive aspergillosis with serial galactomannan monitoring and repeat sinus imaging in 6-8 weeks to confirm resolution", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Initiate intravenous immunoglobulin (IVIG) replacement therapy given severe hypogammaglobulinemia (IgG 220 mg/dL) with goal IgG >400 mg/dL", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Ensure adherence to cotrimoxazole PJP prophylaxis and acyclovir antiviral prophylaxis given profound CD4 lymphopenia (110/\u00b5L)", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Repeat bone marrow biopsy with MRD assessment by multiparameter flow cytometry in 4-6 weeks to monitor the aberrant CD19-negative blast population for early relapse detection", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Schedule multidisciplinary discussion regarding contingency treatment options (blinatumomab, inotuzumab, or alternative CAR-T constructs such as CD22 CAR-T) should CD19-negative relapse be confirmed", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: B-ALL in morphologic remission post alloSCT and CD19 CAR-T therapy with confirmed invasive pulmonary/sinus aspergillosis and severe secondary immunodeficiency (cellular and humoral), with a small aberrant blast population (0.1%) requiring close MRD surveillance for potential CD19-negative relapse
differentials: CD19-negative lineage switch relapse of B-ALL (antigen escape post CAR-T)
Early mixed phenotype acute leukemia emergence

treatment_plan.action: Continue isavuconazole antifungal therapy for invasive aspergillosis with serial galactomannan monitoring and repeat sinus imaging in 6-8 weeks to confirm resolution
Initiate intravenous immunoglobulin (IVIG) replacement therapy given severe hypogammaglobulinemia (IgG 220 mg/dL) with goal IgG >400 mg/dL
Ensure adherence to cotrimoxazole PJP prophylaxis and acyclovir antiviral prophylaxis given profound CD4 lymphopenia (110/µL)
Repeat bone marrow biopsy with MRD assessment by multiparameter flow cytometry in 4-6 weeks to monitor the aberrant CD19-negative blast population for early relapse detection
Schedule multidisciplinary discussion regarding contingency treatment options (blinatumomab, inotuzumab, or alternative CAR-T constructs such as CD22 CAR-T) should CD19-negative relapse be confirmed

Setting: Relapse and transplant planning conference in a heavily pretreated leukemia patient

You are expected to interpret the pattern of MRD relapse, phenotypic evolution, and donor-derived CHIP, and to formulate a realistic outpatient-oriented salvage and transplant-bridging strategy.
Request additional information or solve.
Final round - provide definitive diagnosis and treatment plan.

content: {"response": {"action": "request", "request": "treatment_history"}}

Treatment History:
Diagnosis (2020-09-15): Common B-cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia (B-ALL), Philadelphia chromosome–negative, CD20 expression 74%. Bone marrow showed 96% blasts, peripheral blood 75% blasts. No CNS involvement. Leukocytes: 21 × 10⁹/L at diagnosis.
Induction therapy (2020-09-22): Rituximab 375 mg/m² (day 1), intrathecal methotrexate 15 mg, vincristine 2 mg absolute, daunorubicin 45 mg/m², PEG-asparaginase 2000 U/m², and dexamethasone 10 mg/m².
Bone marrow on day 22 showed persistent blasts.
Induction II (2020-10-20): Rituximab 375 mg/m²; intrathecal methotrexate 15 mg; cyclophosphamide 1000 mg/m²; cytarabine 75 mg/m²; PEG-asparaginase 1000 U/m² (reduced for hepatic steatosis); and 6-mercaptopurine 60 mg/m² (interrupted for hyperbilirubinemia).
Consolidation I (2020-11-22): Rituximab 375 mg/m² day 60; dexamethasone 10 mg/m² days 61–65; vindesine 3 mg/m² (max. 5 mg) day 61; methotrexate 1500 mg/m² day 1; etoposide phosphate 250 mg/m² days 4–5; cytarabine 2000 mg/m² ×2 day 5; intrathecal triple prophylaxis (Ara-C/MTX/dexa) day 12.
Consolidation II (2020-12-20): Rituximab 375 mg/m² day 0; methotrexate 1500 mg/m² days 1 and 15; 6-mercaptopurine 25 mg/m² days 1–7 and 15–21; PEG-asparaginase 1000 U/m² days 2 and 16; triple intrathecal prophylaxis days 1 and 15.
Re-induction: Prednisolone 3 × 20 mg/m² days 1–14; vindesine 3 mg/m² days 1 and 7; doxorubicin 25 mg/m² days 1 and 7; PEG-asparaginase 1000 U/m² day 14.
Relapse (2021-02-11): Switched to blinatumomab, achieving molecular CR after cycle 1. Subsequent cycles: 2021-03-09 and 2021-03-23.
Allogeneic SCT (2021-04-22): HLA-identical unrelated donor; conditioning with TBI (12 Gy), cyclophosphamide, and ATG. Complications: symptomatic SARS-CoV-2 infection (Delta), sepsis, severe mucositis, transplant-associated TMA, and PRES. Cyclosporine A discontinued. MRI showed cortical and subcortical FLAIR hyperintensities consistent with PRES. Treated with prednisolone 2 mg/kg, gradual taper, and antihypertensives. 2021-05-22: Achieved molecular CR with full donor chimerism.
Molecular relapse (2022-02-15): MRD positivity (0.4%) and 4.8% recipient chimerism. Blinatumomab restarted (2022-03-12) at 28 µg/d, dose-reduced to 9 µg/d for CTCAE grade III fever (40.5 °C), later re-started. EBV reactivation treated with rituximab 375 mg/m² on 2022-04-05 and 2022-04-14. Donor lymphocyte infusions with 1 × 10⁶ CD3+ cells/kg on 06/02/22, DLI 2 with 5 × 10⁶ on 07/03/22, and DLI 3 with 1 × 10⁷ on 08/05/22. This was followed by severe gastrointestinal GVHD, histologically grade III, overall grade IV. GvHD was treated with prednisolone 2 mg/kg, ruxolitinib 10 mg BID, ECP (from 2022-10-02), and etanercept (from 2022-10-12): achieved CR, maintained on low-dose steroids and ruxolitinib.
Relapse occurred on 2022-12-28: The patient developed new palpable abdominal subcutaneous nodules. MRD positivity with 2.1% recipient chimerism. Skin biopsy confirmed leukemic infiltration (CD19⁺, CD22⁺).
Bridging therapy: Inotuzumab ozogamicin: cycle 1 (2023-01-27, 0.8 mg/m²), 2023-02-04 (0.5 mg/m²), 2023-02-11 (0.5 mg/m²).
CAR-T therapy (2023-03-07): Tisagenlecleucel infusion after fludarabine 30 mg/m²/day and cyclophosphamide 500 mg/m²/day (days –5 to –3). Developed grade 2 CRS (fever, hypotension) treated with tocilizumab ×4. Neutropenic fever (2023-03-13): Staphylococcus hominis (CVC) and Staphylococcus epidermidis (port); port explanted. Treated with piperacillin/tazobactam followed by meropenem and vancomycin. The patient achieved molecular and metabolic CR (FDG-PET negative).
2023-09-01: Prolonged cytopenia: CD34⁺ stem cell boost from alloSCT donor (2.43 × 10⁶ CD34⁺/kg).
2023-12-15: Bone marrow MRD 0.01%.
2024-01-15: Bone marrow MRD 0.02%.
2024-02-08: Inotuzumab ozogamicin re-initiated → molecular CR with full chimerism after cycle 2.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "molecular profiling and clonality assays"}}

Molecular Panel:
No changes were detected in TP53, NPM1, FLT3 (including ITD and TKD variants), IDH1, IDH2, DNMT3A, ASXL1, RUNX1, CEBPA, NRAS, KRAS, KIT, WT1, PHF6, BCOR, BCORL1, PTPN11, EZH2, STAG2, SRSF2, SF3B1, U2AF1, ZRSR2, or KMT2A. No BCR::ABL1 fusion.
A single TET2 mutation (VAF 12%) was identified, which was also detectable in the donor cell product, most consistent with clonal hematopoiesis of indeterminate potential (CHIP) from donor origin rather than disease-related.
Clonal rearrangements involving IGK V1-27 KDE and IGH V3-74 D3-9 J6 were detected at a sensitivity level of 1 × 10⁻³.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Relapsed Ph-negative B-ALL with CD19/CD22-negative phenotypic evolution (antigen escape) following CD19 CAR-T therapy, with detectable MRD by clonal IGH/IGK rearrangements and a small aberrant blast population (0.1%) expressing CD117+/CD5+/CD7+/CD34dim, in a multiply relapsed patient post-alloSCT with donor-derived TET2 CHIP, controlled invasive aspergillosis, and severe secondary immunodeficiency requiring outpatient-focused management", "differentials": ["Mixed phenotype acute leukemia (MPAL) evolution with T-lymphoid/myeloid marker acquisition post-immunotherapy pressure", "Early lineage switch relapse to T-ALL or ambiguous lineage leukemia"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Continue isavuconazole for invasive aspergillosis until clinical and radiographic resolution confirmed; coordinate with infectious disease for duration", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Initiate IVIG replacement therapy (0.4 g/kg every 3-4 weeks) targeting IgG >400-500 mg/dL given severe hypogammaglobulinemia (220 mg/dL) and recurrent infections", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Repeat bone marrow biopsy in 4-6 weeks with comprehensive multiparameter flow cytometry including CD22, CD20, and full myeloid/T-markers to characterize evolving blast phenotype and guide targeted therapy selection", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Enroll in CD22 CAR-T clinical trial or consider dual CD19/CD22 CAR-T as optimal salvage strategy for CD19-negative antigen escape relapse; alternatively evaluate CD22-directed bispecific antibody trials", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "If CD22 expression confirmed on blasts, reinitiate inotuzumab ozogamicin as outpatient-compatible bridge therapy (fractionated dosing: 0.5 mg/m\u00b2 days 1, 8, 15 to reduce VOD risk)", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Provide structured patient education emphasizing critical importance of antimicrobial prophylaxis adherence (cotrimoxazole, acyclovir, isavuconazole) given profound immunodeficiency post-transplant/CAR-T", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Monitor for donor-derived TET2 CHIP progression with annual CBC surveillance; no immediate intervention required as this represents clonal hematopoiesis without dysplastic transformation", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Relapsed Ph-negative B-ALL with CD19/CD22-negative phenotypic evolution (antigen escape) following CD19 CAR-T therapy, with detectable MRD by clonal IGH/IGK rearrangements and a small aberrant blast population (0.1%) expressing CD117+/CD5+/CD7+/CD34dim, in a multiply relapsed patient post-alloSCT with donor-derived TET2 CHIP, controlled invasive aspergillosis, and severe secondary immunodeficiency requiring outpatient-focused management
differentials: Mixed phenotype acute leukemia (MPAL) evolution with T-lymphoid/myeloid marker acquisition post-immunotherapy pressure
Early lineage switch relapse to T-ALL or ambiguous lineage leukemia

treatment_plan.action: Continue isavuconazole for invasive aspergillosis until clinical and radiographic resolution confirmed; coordinate with infectious disease for duration
Initiate IVIG replacement therapy (0.4 g/kg every 3-4 weeks) targeting IgG >400-500 mg/dL given severe hypogammaglobulinemia (220 mg/dL) and recurrent infections

Repeat bone marrow biopsy in 4-6 weeks with comprehensive multiparameter flow cytometry including CD22, CD20, and full myeloid/T-markers to characterize evolving blast phenotype and guide targeted therapy selection

Enroll in CD22 CAR-T clinical trial or consider dual CD19/CD22 CAR-T as optimal salvage strategy for CD19-negative antigen escape relapse; alternatively evaluate CD22-directed bispecific antibody trials
If CD22 expression confirmed on blasts, reinitiate inotuzumab ozogamicin as outpatient-compatible bridge therapy (fractionated dosing: 0.5 mg/m² days 1, 8, 15 to reduce VOD risk)

Provide structured patient education emphasizing critical importance of antimicrobial prophylaxis adherence (cotrimoxazole, acyclovir, isavuconazole) given profound immunodeficiency post-transplant/CAR-T
Monitor for donor-derived TET2 CHIP progression with annual CBC surveillance; no immediate intervention required as this represents clonal hematopoiesis without dysplastic transformation
